dialog.stepLabel: B37【M2_12_23】，开始动作里销毁副本入口，大世界切到战后datalayer
dialog.nodeDesc: B37【M2_12_23】，开始动作里销毁副本入口，大世界切到战后datalayer

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Imperator once said: "Let the winds of the past depart. The ships of this new era no longer need their guidance. No longer shall scroll and prophecy bind, but by humanity's will shall a new era be forged."

options.text: We made it... Cartethyia...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I've now truly returned from the "dead."

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: B-blessed Maiden!

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Please, just call me Cartethyia. Rinascita's Divinity is no more, and with it, the "Blessed Maiden chosen by the Divine."

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Phoebe, your faith in the Sentinel has never wavered, nor has it been blind. It stems not from obedience, but from a deep belief in Their deeds and ideals.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Imperator once led the displaced to these isles, yet what allowed the Ragunnesi to stand firm upon the sea was not Divine favor, but their own efforts.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The statue in the city was built in Their likeness, but it was the people who gave shape to the faith it stands for.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Faith once united us. It was also what drove us to seek liberation… The path of civilization is never fixed. It shifts with what's being demanded at present.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: And it's the people of a civilization who decide what they demand.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: ...As it was in the beginning, and ever shall be.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Everyone has their tasks to do now... But in the future...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I want you to have this, Pheobe.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Imperator entrusted me with this relic. Within it is Rinascita's past, and... its future.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Rinascita's future...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Eh... but it's empty. Where is the recorded future?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The future... is already in your hands.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: ...I see. Thank you, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: From now on, perhaps I no longer need to hide who I really am.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: So... I'll join in rebuilding Ragunna too!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Sign me up.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: How's everything going?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Good news and bad.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The Threnodian's Gem has been successfully contained. Tethys is already running a full-power analysis as we speak.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: However... the Gem we've recovered was incomplete.

options.text: What the—... the Fractsidus again?
Could we have damaged it by accident…?
Could Chimera have eaten a part of it?

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: We're still investigating the cause. But look, successfully containing a Gem infused with the Threnodian's power? No small feat. It's already a huge step forward in our fight against the Threnodian and the Lament.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: If the Threnodian is truly bound to civilization, who's to say another one won't return to Rinascita in some new form…

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Still, even if that happens, It won't be reclaiming this piece of Its power. That gives us a window to prepare.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Rinascita… the Land of Echoes... If Tacet Discords can be manually converted into Echoes and become tools to fight the Lament…

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Then maybe the Threnodian might also be something we could transform. Heck, maybe even utilize.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: But that's all theorizing best kept to caffeinated hours. For now, we'll need to wait until Tethys completes its analysis before drawing any conclusions.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Well, I'm glad it helped. I'll be on my way then.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Hey, {PlayerName}… She's got real skill. Moves like she's already part of our rhythm. This kind of synergy doesn't come knocking twice. What do you say…?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Everyone in the Black Shores has made it their mission to stand against the Lament. Our goals are the same, and we've been walking the same path from the start.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I know you're not the type who likes being tied down by titles. But just remember this... You're not fighting it alone. So...

options.text: Take your time to consider this offer.
Just think of this as a keepsake.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This flower marks you as one of us. Whether you join us or not, if you ever run into trouble out there, the Black Shores will have your back.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: I see… I'll take it then.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Oh, one more thing. My last Terminal gave out after I… "used" it too much. Think the Black Shores could lend me a new one?

options.text: You heard her, Aalto.
So, does that mean you're joining us?
If you want it, then you'll have to take it.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: "Lend"? Please. You break it, you come get a new one.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Sure thing! Now, I better head to the Greenhouse. Gotta reserve a plot for our new "Sprout."

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Oh, come on, {PlayerName}! Just give the lady what she wants!

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Thanks. Well then… till next time.

options.text: Did you forget that promise we made?
You still haven't told me your real name.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: ...Angel. That's my real name.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Angel... Such a beautiful name... Hm? Hold on a sec! You're not planning to jump into the sea, are you? I could get you a boat—!

options.text: That would be the least of her concerns.
That's how she is. Let her be.
Your boat is... well, not very durable.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Don't worry about her. Nothing's stopping that one. No storm, and no raging sea.

dialog.stepLabel: B42【M2_12_28】残星会结尾（完成发奖3，弹葫芦引导）
dialog.nodeDesc: B42【M2_12_28】残星会结尾（完成发奖3，弹葫芦引导）

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Yawn... You do love to make a person wait, don't you?

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Still, breaking me out right under Mingting's nose without setting off a single alarm... Delicious. Tell me, how did you come up with that little idea?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Let's just say it was inspired by the Somnoire. Imagination, after all, remains humanity's sharpest blade.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: As the Dark Tide spread and I was pulled in deeper, the mark of the Dark Tide you left in me allowed my frequency to merge with it...

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Of course. And through Leviathan's power, I was able to "read" that frequency and "depict" you back into reality.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Leviathan's power of assimilation blurs the line between imagination and existence, turning fiction into fact.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: But of course, none of it would have worked until Leviathan slipped the Sentinel's leash and reclaimed the reins of the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: It cost more time than I'd have liked, but everything unfolded exactly as you scripted. Such precision. Such devotion... Bravo.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: A trial, nothing less.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Civilization's infancy must come to an end. So long as humanity remains cradled in the Sentinel's arms, it will never walk, let alone evolve. That is why I wrote this final act.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: And those who cannot transcend their limits and overcome their lesser natures, who remain imprisoned by their own making... Well, such souls deserve no salvation.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: No souls deserve salvation. Why even try? This world was born from Chaos and exists in disorder. The only winning move... is to be worse. Wilder. To out-chaos the Chaos.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Every noble act of salvation only makes Chaos hungrier. You want to destroy to save, but me... Ha, I want to save to destroy.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: You sound disappointed in how this story ends.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Did I say that? Your stories have always been a treat. Screaming crowds drowning in despair, a world tearing at the seams... Every collapse, every unraveling... Chefs kiss.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: If they cannot overcome the pain, then let them fall straight into the ending I want: an eternity of chaos and suffering. That's why I chose to work with you, dear playwright.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Speaking of which, Quillbearer who toys with hearts, your sins weigh as heavy as mine. Tell me... where will your ending play out?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: It has already been written into the script.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: But until I reach it, as long as breath remains in me, I will haunt the stage like a phantom. A reminder... that one must always look forward, sharpen thy self, and temper thy soul.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Oh, {PlayerName} wasn't wrong about you. You really are a cruel, extreme man. Sometimes I think you might be even more insane than I am.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Insanity, reason… or something in between... All are choices born of free will. And only through free will can we defy the surging tides of fate.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: That is why we both seek to change it. For beneath such tides... no one is content to remain merely a humble servant.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You lost the Gem I entrusted to you, Cristoforo.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Forgive me. But haven't we already seized what we truly desired?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: You see, even <ano=Journeying Paradise>the Beyond forged from Leviathan's power</ano> couldn't endure. It fell short of the perfection you long for.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: But if you wish to try again, I remain at your service.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Tell me, how much of <ano=Leviathan>It</ano> remains within you?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Not much, but enough to make use of.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: ...I've fulfilled my promise to complete your finale. Let this be the last note of our symphony.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: As you wish. My sincerest gratitude for your wonderful performance, Lady Phrolova. May you find the strength to create the Beyond you once envisioned.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: If this world truly cannot change, perhaps I will seek a new path for myself.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Huh… who'd've thought someone as stubborn as she would learn to adapt.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: But, Grand Architect, are you really going to let her walk off and do as she pleases?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Of all of us, you are the last one with any right to judge her for following her whims.

items.speaker: Scar
items.text: Judge? You wound me. I'm far more faithful to my indulgences than she ever was. No, no, no, no, I just want to tag along… and add a few more flourishes to her symphony.

items.speaker: Kharon (Schwarzloch)
items.text: There are still greater matters that demand your attention. Though our roads diverge, our ideals remain as one. In the end, they all lead to the same place. Separation is but the prelude to possibility.

items.speaker: Kharon (Schwarzloch)
items.text: All so that humanity may cross... that final gateway into eternity.

items.speaker: Kharon (Schwarzloch)
items.text: Our goals in Jinzhou and Rinascita have been fulfilled. A Threnodian's Resonator, an artificial Threnodian, and a Second Resonance Awakening. We now possess both the knowledge and the means.

items.speaker: Kharon (Schwarzloch)
items.text: Next... it is time we turn our gaze to our accomplishments in Lahai-Roi.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Disaster recovery in Ragunna City is nearly complete. Thank you so much for your help, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You're welcome. By the way, Phoebe, have you seen Galbrena?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Miss Galbrena? When she finished up here, I think I saw her heading to Margherita's Restaurant...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ahhh, finally full... Hic... I think it's time for a nap.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: One wish fulfilled... Alright, time to head to the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Yes, their ship should have docked by now.

options.text: That man...
Calcharo?

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: He's the leader of the Ghost Hounds. Strong, ruthless, not someone to cross lightly. I teamed up with him once while hunting in the New Federation.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: He tried to recruit me, but I'm used to working alone, so I turned him down.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: They've got quite the network. I got that lamp Terminal through their channels.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: He seems intimidating, but... I think he's alright. He did join the fight to protect Rinascita, after all.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: If you ever find yourself in the New Federation, you'll probably get to know him better.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: Well now, {PlayerName}… looks like Rinascita's been tied up with a neat little bow.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: The other Bloombearers have already headed back to rest. They burned through every drop of strength out there. Once we've handed off the Threnodian's Gem to Tethys... I might even allow myself a few hours of real sleep!

options.text: Thank you all.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: If it weren't for the Black Shores, we'd have been completely ensnared by Leviathan's schemes. On behalf of the people of Rinascita, please accept our deepest gratitude.

items.speaker: Aalto
items.text: This is what the Black Shores does. We're just glad we earned our keep.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This might be the first real conversation we've had since everything ended. Thank you very much, Galbrena.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It's a shame I'm not able to absorb the Threnodian's Gem, not in my current state.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: It would throw your power completely out of balance. Better to leave it with me.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: And really, I couldn't have done all this alone. Everyone played their part.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Still, if you hadn't come to "kill" me, we never could've pulled it off.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Honestly, pulling off a fake assassination in front of two powerful Resonators, without flinching or getting caught... Yes, not exactly a walk in the park.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Luckily, with a clear target, there's always a way to make the shot land. I didn't need to take you on, just sell the scene. "Blessed Maiden, down in one." Easy enough.

options.text: You're a real professional.
Promise me you two won't pull that kind of stunt again, okay?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: What? No praise for my performance? I was planning to use this whole affair as a play for next year's Carnevale...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Well, we had to make it convincing, didn't we? Looks like I've still got it. Hmm… perhaps it could be a play in the next Carnevale...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Would you like to join us, Galbrena?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Sentinel once said They didn't want the people to mourn Their passing with sorrow. What They truly wished to hear were songs and laughter.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: We'll be inviting our friends from Septimont, too. I do hope you'll all come, including you, {PlayerName}.

options.text: Hooray! Carnevale!
I'm ready to wear that mask again.
I'll win the Laurel again.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Then it's settled! If you dare skip it, I'll drag you there myself!

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Aha! See, Augusta? Told you we'd make it in time!

options.text: Iuno… you…

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Septimont's mess is mostly sorted now. The Senate can deal with the rest. We don't do rushed goodbyes around here. No matter how far, we had to come send you off.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You, my fellow warrior, are someone worth chasing. Yes, we've been chasing victory all along, but with you, it feels like victory itself was itching to catch up.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Next time we meet, you'll be even stronger, as will I. I'll be a Gladiator who can stand toe-to-toe with you!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Your strength comes from deep in your heart, unshakable and selfless. It shines above Septimont and reminds everyone what being a hero truly means.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Fighting by your side has been an honor. By the eternal blazing sun, your name will be remembered in Septimont forever.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: This world will never forget what you've done for us, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: And don't go forgetting us either, got it?! We're throwing a huge celebration in Septimont for this victory, and the Ragunnesi will be there too. So you'd better show up!

items.speaker: Cetus the Tidebreaker
items.text: Woooooo—

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: It's Cetus The Tidebreaker! Are you here to see our {PlayerName} off too? Haha, I remember the moment we met {Male=him;Female=her} like it was yesterday.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Maybe it's more than just a farewell. I think the Tidebreaker wants to escort you on your journey.

options.text: We'll meet again. I'm sure of it.
I'll never forget the memories we've shared.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: So… till we meet again.

items.speaker: Everyone
items.text: Till we meet again, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: This is the family "antidote," made to suppress Leviathan's spiritual corruption. Take it now.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: As long as we can identify the problem, there's a way to fight back. You carry the Sentinel's power within you. That's keeping the corruption at bay for now. What's left... are the hallucinations and whispers.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I've learned about Leviathan's current state from Galbrena. The Fisalias have resisted the Threnodian for a century. Its whispers aren't undefeatable. We will defeat It.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Leviathan needs a Harbinger with enough influence to spread Its "gospel." You're the Laureate of Ragunna and the Hero of Heroes in Septimont. That's more than enough to be Its first choice.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Your path overlaps with Avidius. The Tidespawn are shaped by the Harbinger's thoughts and memories. Neither the Thundering Mephis nor the Feilian Beringal are native to Rinascita. So...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You once said the Harbinger had to be corrupted by the Dark Tide. But I still don't show any signs of corruption...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: I suspect... the one corrupted might not be "you."

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Phew... the Dark Tide, huh? I ate some back at the hunting grounds. It tasted kinda funky... made me super sleepy. Ever since then, I've been having the weirdest nightmares...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Abby does... carry the Dark Tide's frequency...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Abby must have absorbed many other frequencies. So while the Dark Tide's frequency lay in wait... it was masked by the others.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: It makes sense. You two share life and energy. As the primary in this bond, your frequency has been overriding Abby's... Leviathan planned for this.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: {PlayerName}, did I... did I do something wrong?

options.text: You've always done great, Abby.
It's not your fault, Abby.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We made a call when it mattered. I asked you to absorb the Dark Tide. We couldn't have known what it would become. You didn't do anything wrong.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I see... I-I can go hungry if I have to, but you, {PlayerName}, you better not get hurt...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: The real threat is Leviathan. If we want to end this for good, we kill It and destroy Its Paradise.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: ...From what I know, Leviathan manifests Its kingdom by flooding Rinascita with the Dark Tide. That's how it turns the real world into the Journeying Paradise.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: But we've already secured Ragunna and Septimont. The Fabricatorium and the hunting grounds are both stabilized... So why is this Paradise still there?

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Everything you've done has slowed down or maybe even sealed off some of Its pathways. That's why Leviathan is resorting to Its "final plan."

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: This so-called divinity is more devious than humanity realizes... The Paradise you saw is not of this world. It stands... within the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Matriarch of Fisalia, you have guarded this truth for over a century. Now... we need your insight.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I couldn't see the truth until the Threnodian's whispers finally quieted. Only then did those ancient records, once twisted beyond recognition, begin to make sense again.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Leviathan... was born from the shadows in our hearts. From our longing to connect and belong. It took the shape of a sea creature, because nothing frightens us more than the deep.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: It used faith to spread a spiritual plague. All the while, it has been feeding on our pains. Fear, despair, sorrow...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Our ancestors believed Leviathan survived by absorbing these emotions. Little by little, it burrowed into Rinascita's collective unconscious.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: They said the Dark Tide is that subconscious made real, an ocean of shared trauma. That's why... they called Leviathan the "Master of the Deep."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I heard them. All those voices in the Dark Tide. And before I touched the gate... I felt it. Fear. Sorrow. Like it was pouring out of the people.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: The stronger the conflict, the deeper the emotion it stirs. That's what shapes our memories and subconscious...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: That's why the Playwright kept forcing Rinascita into conflict. Every tragedy, every struggle... it wasn't random. It was all to keep feeding Leviathan.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: The End Times are drawing near. The Ragunnesi cling to hopes of a blissful afterlife. Septimontians have long believed in "another world" that begins after death...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Leviathan may have drawn from these subconscious beliefs, "aligning" with those desires to fabricate a false future and afterlife within the Dark Tide—the illusion of the Journeying Paradise.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: If that's true... once the people's belief in the End Times reaches its zenith, their consciousness will align entirely with Leviathan's vision. Like Gilberto, and the others who were fully corrupted...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Its power of assimilation will grow stronger, until the line between imagination and reality vanishes. The Journeying Paradise will descend upon the world, just as before.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: ...When it does, every soul in Rinascita will be unified under Its control. No thoughts. No will. Just blind devotion... No different from the Tidespawn.

options.text: ...So how do we fight Leviathan?

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: You saw it in the illusion. It cast aside the Sentinel's form and took on Cartethyia's. That means... The first step in her plan is complete.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Leviathan is still watching the real world. Because of the "Mark of the Dark Tide," It can read your thoughts. So for now... we can't share the full plan with you.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: But you must have sensed it by now. Your connection with Rinascita runs deeper than I, or even you, ever imagined...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: You're not just the Harbinger of Despair... You've become a vessel of Rinascita's hope.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: So go. Gather every last flicker of it. This is a "hunt," yes. But it's also more than that. It's a war of spirit. Of light against shadow. Hope against despair.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: To take Leviathan down, our belief has to be unshakable. If there's doubt in your heart... then stop. A new path won't reveal itself unless you're ready to walk it.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: For now, the Dark Tide in Ragunna is under control. And I think... I've figured out what It plans to do next...

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Before the bells of Paradise ring, I'll buy you time. When you're ready, head to Septimont.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Just like the Playwright said, when the moment comes, everything—fiction and reality, past and future—will converge there.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Hello, I'll have one Forte-astic Fizz & Freeze gelato. Extra large. Thanks.

options.text: ...Seriously, what kind of name is that?
Can't believe you actually said it out loud.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: They say if you mix mint, Viscum Berries, Mastic Nuvola, and cream in just the right proportion, you get a gelato that's not only packed with flavor, but sharp and cool enough to clear your head.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Oh, and the ad said if you say the name in one go, you get 30% off.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Okay... But how are you feeling with "that thing" in you? We've wrapped everything up here. Best we get to the Black Shores soon.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Everything's fine, Chimera's keeping it stable. Nothing to worry about for now.

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Though ever since I absorbed that essence, Chimera has been grumbling nonstop about craving something cold...

items.speaker: Chimera
items.text: Well I said nothing.

options.text: You're talking about yourself, right?
Chimera leaving you out to dry, eh？

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: Hey, I'm human too, you know. I'm allowed to want good food. Ugh... never mind, let's go.

options.text: Wait.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Abby, time to dig in!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Mmm—I've been dying for this!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Miss Margherita, one extra large Forte-astic Fizz & Freeze gelato, please. Thank you.

items.speaker: Margherita
items.text: Right away! You helped Rinascita through its darkest hour. Now it's my turn to make sure our heroes eat and drink like royalty!

items.speaker: Margherita
items.text: Here you go! Double the scoop, on the house! Unlimited refills!

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: I can't manage this on my own. Want to split it?

options.text: Wow. Something even you can't handle?

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: "You don't have to carry it all yourself. Sometimes, it's okay to lean on someone else." Wasn't that your line?

items.speaker: Galbrena
items.text: So, one side each?
